source.title: Humans use directed and random exploration to solve the explore-exploit dilemma

confidence_rationale: Direct behavioural-modeling demonstration in humans (Princeton lab) that BOTH directed information-seeking AND random decision noise contribute to exploration; horizon manipulation dissociates the two. Highest-relevance single paper for R1 (structured-vs-noise verdict) -- the data force the both-channels-needed reading. Confidence not 1.0 because it is a single behavioural paradigm (Horizon task), and primate / lesion replication is more limited than the human modelling.
failure_signatures: Either-or arguments for behavioural diversity (curiosity-only or noise-only) are predicted to fail to explain horizon-dependent shifts in both information-seeking AND decision noise that this study quantifies.

An REE substrate that implements only structured curiosity (no stochastic floor) should fail to reproduce the random-exploration component of the longer-horizon condition; one that implements only noise should fail to reproduce the directed information-seeking component.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Humans solve the explore-exploit dilemma using two strategies in parallel: directed exploration (choices explicitly biased toward information-seeking) and random exploration (decision noise produces exploration by chance). Both contributions scale with horizon length.
mapping.ree_translation: ARC-065 behavioral_diversity_generation_pathway must include BOTH a structured channel (curiosity / information-gain bias on E3 score_bias; candidate MECH-314) AND a stochastic channel (action-policy noise floor; candidate MECH-313). Wilson 2014 falsifies any single-channel reading of the substrate. R1 verdict: (c) both-channels-needed.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The Horizon task measures choice in a discrete N-armed bandit with explicit horizon information; REE agents face a continuous-state grid environment without explicit horizon awareness. The qualitative dual-channel finding is robust across many task variants in the Wilson lab and others, but the magnitudes of the two channels' contributions in REE will need to be empirically calibrated; Wilson's parameter values do not transfer directly.
mapping.source_context: Horizon task with N-armed bandit, healthy adult human participants, Princeton Neuroscience Institute (Wilson, Geana, White, Ludvig, Cohen).

confidence_components.notes: JEP General is a top-tier behavioural-psychology venue; methods are clean and the model-fitting is rigorous. Mapping fidelity is high for the qualitative both-channels claim; transfer risk is moderate because the bandit-with-known-horizon paradigm differs from REE's open-ended grid environment, and the relative weight of directed vs random channels is task-dependent.
